_comment: Wang Qingyue is the village trader. Trading is fundamentally transactional — goods for currency, not favors. Her motivation system (m_seek_profitable_trade) operates on margins, not obligations. However, if the player brings rare items that enable an especially profitable trade deal, or shares a valuable market rumor, an informal minor obligation (owed_to_player) could form — the trader's version of 'I owe you one, next time you need something hard to find, I will look.' At chapter start: empty. All interactions are purely commercial.

aggregates._comment: Starts at zero. Wang Qingyue's favors, when they form, are uniquely practical: 'next time you come to trade, I will set aside the rare reagent you need before anyone else sees it.' They are minor in magnitude but highly useful. A trader's favor is access, not money.
